MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRUISE LLC'S MOTION TO ADMIT AMY BICE LARSON COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion for pro hac vice admission of Amy Bice Larson is granted. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To May-18-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Sportsmed Physical Therapy Llc To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For Medical Records; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Sportsmed Physical Therapy Llc In The Sum Of $2,280.00
Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CRUISE LLC's Motion To Sportsmed Physical Therapy Llc To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For Medical Records; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Sportsmed Physical Therapy Llc In The Sum Of $2,280.00.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cruise LLC's motion to compel compliance with its deposition subpoena against Sportsmed Physical Therapy LLC is denied. The subpoena was served in New Jersey only after it was domesticated via a New Jersey proceeding. This court exercises jurisdiction only in California. Cruise's moving papers do not address whether the court has jurisdiction in this circumstance to order a New Jersey entity to comply with a New Jersey court's domestication order. The court concludes it does not and that the New Jersey court issuing the domestication order is the proper court to look to for relief.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2024 Set For Trial On Feb-17-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-614871 WITH CASE CGC-24-614415
Off Calendar Nov-06-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHANEL KATIRAIE, Esq. of Setareh Law, APLC (Tel: 310-659-1826) for plaintiff;
Atty. JAMAL J. ATIBA, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendant Lyft, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CRUISE LLC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE NO. CGC-24-614871 WITH CASE NO. CGC-24-614415.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cruise LLC's motion to consolidate case number 614871 with 614415 is granted; the latter is designated the lead case. The two cases involve the same traffic accident. The trial judge has the power to bifurcate trial if she/he deems it appropriate.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-614871 WITH CASE CGC-24-614415

MINI MINUTES:

Atty. CHANEL KATIRAIE, Esq. of Setareh Law APLC (tel: 310-659-1826) for Plaintiff Sisay Berhane Berha.

Atty. JOHN POWERS, Esq. of Faegre Drinker Biddle & Reath LLP (tel: 415-591-7500) for Defendant Cruise LLC.
Atty. ELISE SANGUINETTI, Esq. of Arias Sanguinetti Wang & Torrijos LLP (tel: 510-629-4877) for Plaintiffs Donna Collins and John Collins in the action to be consolidated.
Atty. JAMAL ATIBA, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (tel: 415-652-0041) for Defendant Lyft, Inc. in the action to be consilated.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CRUISE LLC's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-614871 WITH CASE CGC-24-614415.

The Court states it inadvertently failed to acknowledge the timely filed opposition to the motion to consolidate. Upon conclusion, the Court continues the matter to November 6, 2024, in department 302 at 9:30 A.M., to consider the timely filed opposition.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
